automotive-ev-battery.title: Alpitronic increases CCS charging power of HYC1000 system to 1,000kW
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Alpitronic has upgraded its HYC1000 decentralised megawatt charging system to deliver up to 1,000kW of charging power, nearly doubling the previous 600kW maximum. The new high-performance dispenser uses a single liquid-cooled CCS connector capable of supplying electric vehicles with over 1,000 amperes, representing a significant advancement in ultra-fast charging infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: CATL and Octopus Energy plan battery-swapping network for eHGVs in Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese battery manufacturer CATL and British energy supplier Octopus Energy have formed a joint venture called 'Swaptopus' to establish a Europe-wide network of battery-swapping stations for electric heavy-duty trucks. The partnership aims to accelerate the electrification of European commercial transport by enabling rapid battery replacement as an alternative to extended charging times.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Facelift for the Renault Mégane E-Tech – with up to 500km of range
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Renault has unveiled a comprehensive facelift of its Mégane E-Tech Electric featuring a redesigned exterior, an expanded battery pack, increased charging capacity, and extended driving range of up to 500 kilometers. The refresh strengthens Renault's competitive position in the mainstream electric vehicle segment with improved practicality and performance metrics.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Small, electric, and quick to charge: Smart's bid to reinvent the two-seater
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Smart has revealed details about its upcoming #2 electric two-seater built on the proprietary ECA platform, featuring a 35.7-kWh battery, approximately 300-kilometer WLTP range, and sub-20-minute fast charging capability. The company is targeting an entry price below €20,000, positioning the vehicle as an affordable, technically advanced successor to the original Fortwo.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EVs At 33.7% Share In Germany — Skoda Elroq Leads
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Germany's first quarter 2026 results show plugin electric vehicles capturing 33.7 percent market share, up significantly from 26.6 percent in the same period last year, with battery-electric vehicles driving most of the growth. The Skoda Elroq emerged as the best-selling battery-electric vehicle in the German market, underscoring strong consumer demand for mainstream electric models.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Chevrolet launches production of Captiva EV in Brazil
automotive-ev-battery.summary: General Motors has commenced local production of the Chevrolet Captiva EV in Brazil's Ceará state, marking the second all-electric Chevrolet model manufactured in the region following the Spark EUV. The expansion of EV production capacity in Brazil demonstrates General Motors' commitment to electrification in Latin American markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Honda Super-N now available to order in the UK
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Honda has announced UK pricing and opened order books for its new Super-N electric city car with an entry price of £18,995. The competitively priced compact electric vehicle represents Honda's strategy to expand its presence in the affordable EV segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: DP World launches electric HGV rental scheme in the UK
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Port operator DP World has expanded its Low Carbon Truck Programme at Southampton port with a new electric truck rental initiative allowing members to trial electric heavy-goods vehicles for up to twelve weeks. The initiative aims to address barriers to EV adoption in commercial transport by reducing the financial and operational risks associated with fleet conversion.

automotive-ev-battery.title: The EV Battery Call Is Coming From Inside The House
automotive-ev-battery.summary: US-based energy storage startup QuantumScape continues its steady progress toward commercial-scale production of advanced EV battery technology despite recent federal policy shifts regarding electrification support. The company's advancement represents domestic efforts to reduce dependency on foreign battery suppliers and strengthen American EV manufacturing capabilities.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Costa Rica announces $4.6m public EV charging push
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Costa Rica's electricity institute has announced plans to deploy 180 new semi-fast 21-kW public AC charging stations over the next three years as part of a $4.6 million investment initiative. The infrastructure expansion aims to support broader EV adoption in Central America by improving charging accessibility for electric vehicle owners.

science-space.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope arrives in Florida ahead of SpaceX Falcon Heavy launch this summer
science-space.summary: The Nancy Roman Grace Space Telescope has arrived at NASA's Kennedy Space Center for final preparations before its anticipated launch on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket this summer. The observatory will join other flagship missions including Hubble, Webb, and Chandra in orbit, significantly expanding humanity's capabilities for deep space observation and cosmological research.

science-space.title: NASA's Webb Finds Clues to Ancient, Distant Origin of Comet 3I/ATLAS
science-space.summary: Using the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers have analyzed the chemical composition of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS as it receded from the Sun, revealing detailed measurements of its ancient ice composition. The findings provide unprecedented insights into the chemical makeup and origins of material from beyond our solar system.

science-space.title: NASA to Cover US Spacewalk 95, Host Preview News Conference
science-space.summary: NASA astronauts will conduct a spacewalk on June 30 to replace a wrist joint on the International Space Station's Canadarm2 robotic arm, marking a critical maintenance operation for orbital operations. The agency will host a preview news conference with experts from NASA and the Canadian Space Agency to discuss the mission details.

science-space.title: NASA's Chandra Finds Possible Supernova Remnant
science-space.summary: Using NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory, astronomers have identified a possible supernova remnant in the Milky Way's central region, providing evidence of ancient stellar explosions. Supernova remnants distribute critical elements including iron, oxygen, and silicon throughout the galaxy, enriching cosmic material that eventually forms new stars and planets.

science-space.title: Dark energy is still accelerating the expansion of the universe, and astronomers are relieved
science-space.summary: New research confirms that dark energy continues to accelerate the expansion of the universe, resolving recent claims that challenged this fundamental cosmological model. The findings avert a potential crisis in cosmology and reinforce current understanding of the universe's large-scale structure and evolution.

science-space.title: This ball of stars named Terzan 5 may be one of the Milky Way's original building blocks
science-space.summary: Astronomers have discovered that the globular cluster Terzan 5 possesses unusual properties suggesting it may be a primordial component of the Milky Way galaxy. The cluster's characteristics provide valuable insights into the early formation and structure of our galaxy.

science-space.title: James Webb Space Telescope captures the star-forming clouds of Orion A in stunning detail
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has captured unprecedented high-resolution images of the star-forming regions in Orion A, revealing new detail and vibrant structures in this stellar nursery. The observations provide critical data for understanding how stars form within molecular clouds.

science-space.title: NASA Sounding Rocket to Launch Student Experiments
science-space.summary: NASA's Wallops Flight Facility will launch a sounding rocket on June 24 carrying student-developed experiments as part of the RockSatX and RockOn programs. These initiatives provide undergraduate and high school students with hands-on technical training and real spaceflight experience for scientific research missions.

science-space.title: SpaceX launching its 1st 'Starfall' reentry capsule early on June 23
science-space.summary: SpaceX is conducting the maiden flight of its Starfall spacecraft on June 23, demonstrating the vehicle's operational capabilities in space and its safe return to Earth. The test flight marks a significant milestone in the development of SpaceX's reentry capsule technology.

science-space.title: Our sun is destined to 'kick and spit' its way across the solar system when it dies
science-space.summary: Recent scientific research reveals that dying red giant stars eject blobs of plasma as they evolve, producing a corresponding recoil effect that alters their trajectories. This discovery provides new insights into the dynamic final stages of stellar evolution and the fate of our own sun.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX sheds $400bn in market value as debut rally hits reverse
finance-markets.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX experienced a significant market valuation decline of over $400 billion following a 16% share tumble, driven by rising US bond yields that have reversed the company's initial public market rally. The sharp correction reflects broader market concerns about valuations in the technology and aerospace sectors amid higher interest rate expectations.

finance-markets.title: Apollo's flagship private credit fund hit by 17% redemption requests
finance-markets.summary: Apollo Global Management's marquee private credit fund is experiencing significant investor outflows, with redemption requests reaching 17% that the fund can only partially meet, fulfilling less than one-third of withdrawal requests. The accelerating exodus signals growing investor concerns about private credit valuations and liquidity in an increasingly volatile market environment.

finance-markets.title: 'FOMO Really Got Me': Taiwanese Go Deep Into Debt to Amp 100% Stock Rally
finance-markets.summary: Taiwan's retail investors are accumulating significant debt to capitalize on an AI-fueled stock rally, particularly in TSMC shares that have surged 100%, raising concerns about potential market bubble conditions. The leveraged buying spree reflects widespread retail investor participation driven by fear of missing out on the semiconductor and artificial intelligence boom.

finance-markets.title: Iran Oil Waiver Offers Lifeline to Tehran as Talks Proceed
finance-markets.summary: The United States issued a 60-day license permitting Iran to sell oil on international markets, providing Tehran with economic relief while peace negotiations continue between the two nations. The waiver represents a significant development in ongoing diplomatic efforts to resolve geopolitical tensions affecting global energy markets.

finance-markets.title: US-Japan More Aligned on FX, Katayama Says After Bessent Talks
finance-markets.summary: Japan's Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama reported improved alignment with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on foreign exchange policy following their telephone discussion, though the yen saw only a brief rally and continues trading near four-decade lows. The dialogue suggests potential coordination between the two nations on currency management amid volatile global markets.

finance-markets.title: Yen Intervention Risk Rises After Katayama-Bessent Talks
finance-markets.summary: Currency traders have heightened vigilance regarding potential Japanese intervention in foreign exchange markets following discussions between Finance Minister Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Bessent, with the yen experiencing continued weakness near four-decade lows. The elevated intervention risk reflects growing market concerns about yen depreciation and potential policy responses from Japanese authorities.

finance-markets.title: Alan Greenspan dies aged 100
finance-markets.summary: Alan Greenspan, the influential Federal Reserve chairman whose tenure shaped decades of US monetary policy, has passed away at age 100. While celebrated during his leadership, Greenspan's legacy underwent significant reassessment following the 2008 financial crisis, with critics pointing to his regulatory approach as contributing to market instability.

finance-markets.title: Oil Steadies as Iran Sales Waiver Signals Peace Talks Progress
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices stabilized after declining more than 3% in the previous session, following the US waiver allowing Iranian oil sales and early indications of progress in peace negotiations. The relative stability in crude reflects market relief regarding potential resolution of geopolitical tensions affecting global energy supply.

finance-markets.title: CFTC Seeks Input on 24/7 Futures, Energy Perpetuals Trades
finance-markets.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is soliciting public comments on expanding energy derivatives trading to include perpetual contracts and 24/7 market access, responding to industry concerns about offshore platforms and excessive leverage. The regulatory review reflects policymakers' focus on balancing market innovation with systemic risk management in derivatives markets.

finance-markets.title: 'A Lot of Leeway': SpaceX's High-Grade Debt Brings Out Skeptics
finance-markets.summary: Credit rating analysts are expressing skepticism about SpaceX's investment-grade debt classification, questioning the company's leverage relative to comparable firms such as Nvidia, which maintained lighter debt burdens during its early public years. The scrutiny reflects concerns about whether SpaceX's debt profile adequately reflects the risks associated with its capital-intensive business operations.

world-news.title: Iran war live: US waives oil sanctions, to release $12bn in frozen funds
world-news.summary: The United States has announced it will waive oil sanctions against Iran and release $12 billion in frozen funds following Iran's commitment to allow international nuclear inspections. This major shift in US-Iran relations represents a significant diplomatic development with implications for regional stability and global energy markets.

world-news.title: US eases oil sanctions as Iran denies Vance claim on nuclear inspectors
world-news.summary: Iran's foreign ministry disputes claims made by US officials regarding new commitments on nuclear inspections, even as the US eases sanctions. The disagreement highlights ongoing tensions in nuclear diplomacy between Washington and Tehran.

world-news.title: Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas
world-news.summary: Russian forces are consolidating military positions around Kostyantynivka, a strategic city whose fall would enable Moscow to advance toward Ukraine's remaining strongholds in the eastern Donbas region. The escalating military pressure represents a critical juncture in the ongoing conflict.

world-news.title: Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says
world-news.summary: A United Nations report documents that Myanmar's military killed 702 civilians over a six-month period, including 153 children, representing a severe humanitarian crisis. The findings underscore ongoing allegations of mass atrocities in the country.

world-news.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
world-news.summary: A technical accident at Qatar's main liquified natural gas processing facility in Ras Laffan killed at least 13 people and injured numerous others. The incident affects one of the world's largest LNG producers and has implications for global energy supplies.

world-news.title: Red heat alerts issued in France, Italy and Spain as 40C temperatures forecast
world-news.summary: Western and central Europe faces an intensifying heatwave with temperatures reaching 40 degrees Celsius across France, Italy, and Spain, prompting official heat warnings. The event reflects broader climate patterns affecting the continent.

world-news.title: US House committee reaches bipartisan deal on social media rules for kids
world-news.summary: A US House committee has reached a bipartisan agreement on legislation designed to hold technology companies accountable for social media usage among children. The deal represents a significant step toward federal regulation of Big Tech platforms.

world-news.title: Kenya minister found in contempt of court over US-backed Ebola centre
world-news.summary: Kenya's Health Minister Aden Duale was found in contempt of court for defying an order to halt construction on a US-funded Ebola research facility. The case involves legal disputes over the controversial medical research project.

world-news.title: How women steer youths away from gang violence in northeastern Nigeria
world-news.summary: Women-led community initiatives in northeastern Nigeria are reducing gang violence by engaging young people and shifting their perspectives away from conflict. These grassroots efforts address the region's persistent security challenges through social intervention.

world-news.title: Largest ever cocaine bust in Australia after police raid underground bunker
world-news.summary: Australian police seized 2.7 tonnes of cocaine valued at approximately A$816 million during a raid on an underground bunker in western Sydney, marking the nation's largest cocaine seizure. The operation reflects ongoing efforts to combat international drug trafficking networks.

tech-ai.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
tech-ai.summary: Major technology companies have announced significant workforce reductions in 2026, with artificial intelligence cited as a primary factor in employment decisions. The trend reflects ongoing industry consolidation and operational restructuring driven by AI adoption and efficiency gains.

tech-ai.title: The AI world is getting 'loopy'
tech-ai.summary: Agentic AI systems are advancing toward continuous autonomous operation, with swarms of AI agents authorized to work endlessly in the background without human intervention. This represents a significant escalation in AI autonomy and deployment capabilities.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft has signed a 20-year power purchase agreement with Chevron to supply a new natural gas power plant for data center operations, locking in significant carbon emissions for decades. The deal highlights the environmental trade-offs tech companies are making to meet AI infrastructure demands.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open-source bugs
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI has announced a security-focused program aimed at identifying and fixing vulnerabilities in open-source software projects. The initiative addresses growing cybersecurity concerns within the open-source development community.

tech-ai.title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia's $20B not-acqui-hire deal
tech-ai.summary: AI chip manufacturer Groq has secured $650 million in funding and is rebuilding its executive team following Nvidia's major talent acquisition deal. The company is focusing on its neocloud business segment as it recovers from the significant industry disruption.

tech-ai.title: A new unpatchable flaw in Apple chips opens the door to an iPhone jailbreak
tech-ai.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed an unfixable vulnerability in Apple's chip architecture that enables exploitation and potential jailbreaking of older iPhone models. The flaw demonstrates persistent security challenges in Apple's hardware design that cannot be remedied through software updates.

tech-ai.title: Tata Electronics, a major tech supplier to Apple and Tesla, confirms data breach
tech-ai.summary: Tata Electronics, a critical supplier to major technology companies including Apple and Tesla, has confirmed a security breach affecting its operations. The incident underscores supply chain vulnerabilities as the company expands its role in global technology manufacturing.

tech-ai.title: Tesla pushes back on Autopilot narrative after fatal Texas crash
tech-ai.summary: Tesla has disputed reports regarding its Autopilot system's involvement in a fatal accident in Texas, claiming uncertainty about whether the feature was active or malfunctioning. Final determination of the system's operational status will depend on analysis of vehicle data logs by investigating authorities.

tech-ai.title: Nvidia wants to cut data center water use, but that's not the same as fixing AI's water problem
tech-ai.summary: Nvidia has unveiled a new cooling system designed to reduce water consumption within its data centers, but the innovation does not address AI's largest water usage stemming from fossil fuel power plant operations. The announcement highlights the limited scope of current environmental mitigation efforts in AI infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: Google invests in A24 to build AI movie tools
tech-ai.summary: Google's DeepMind division is investing approximately $75 million in film studio A24 to develop artificial intelligence-powered movie production technologies. The partnership aims to create tools that expand filmmaking capabilities and represents Google's first equity stake in a major entertainment studio.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. Senate passes housing bill that carries four-year ban on a Fed CBDC
crypto-web3.summary: The U.S. Senate has passed legislation that would formally block the Federal Reserve from developing a central bank digital currency for a four-year period. This represents a significant regulatory setback for CBDC research, which has previously remained largely theoretical at the Fed level.

crypto-web3.title: Intercontinental Exchange, OKX expand access to tokenized equities via joint venture co-chaired by former Gov. Cuomo
crypto-web3.summary: Intercontinental Exchange and OKX have launched a joint venture to provide U.S. and international customers access to tokenized equities from ICE futures and NYSE markets. The partnership represents a major institutional push toward bringing traditional financial assets onchain with backing from a major legacy exchange operator.

crypto-web3.title: Securitize and tZERO clash over patents as race to bring Wall Street onchain heats up
crypto-web3.summary: Tokenization leaders Securitize and tZERO are engaged in patent litigation as competition intensifies in the market to tokenize Wall Street assets. The dispute highlights both the growing commercial value of the space and potential legal barriers to institutional adoption.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto's second U.S. lobbying front — tax policy — sees industry push on mining, staking
crypto-web3.summary: Leading crypto advocacy groups have urged the U.S. House tax committee to advance legislation clarifying the tax treatment of mining and staking rewards. The push represents a coordinated regulatory effort to address a major operational uncertainty for industry participants.

crypto-web3.title: Anchorage aims to bring banks onchain with new tokenized deposit platform
crypto-web3.summary: Anchorage has launched a tokenized deposit platform designed to enable financial institutions to offer continuous payments and settlement without replacing their existing core systems. The offering targets institutional adoption by reducing integration friction for traditional banks entering blockchain infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Ric Edelman says crypto's biggest growth story is happening off the price chart
crypto-web3.summary: Industry veteran Ric Edelman contends that institutional adoption and tokenization are accelerating significantly despite continued price pressures on crypto assets. His assessment highlights a divergence between market sentiment and fundamental infrastructure development in the sector.

crypto-web3.title: MoneyGram joins Solana as validator amid stablecoin payment push
crypto-web3.summary: Remittance firm MoneyGram has become a validator on the Solana blockchain, positioning itself to process and secure transactions while expanding its stablecoin-based payment offerings. The move represents mainstream financial services adoption of blockchain infrastructure for payments.

crypto-web3.title: Ether's biggest corporate holders back new Ethereum research hub
crypto-web3.summary: Major Ethereum asset holders including Consensys CEO Joe Lubin, SharpLink, and Bitmine have established Ethlabs, a nonprofit research organization to accelerate network development beyond the Ethereum Foundation. The initiative signals institutional commitment to Ethereum's continued evolution and governance expansion.

crypto-web3.title: 21Shares co-founder warns tokenization hype is outrunning Wall Street reality
crypto-web3.summary: A 21Shares executive has cautioned that enthusiasm for tokenization exceeds the current state of financial infrastructure preparedness for institutional-scale deployment. While acknowledging settlement and asset movement benefits, the warning highlights infrastructure gaps that must be addressed for widespread adoption.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum Foundation talent exodus sparks fresh debate over leadership
crypto-web3.summary: The announced departure of Ethereum Foundation co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang has reignited community discussion about the foundation's leadership structure and future direction. The resignation follows a pattern of recent departures that raises questions about organizational governance and strategy.

health-biotech.title: FDA to launch pilot program to speed up early-stage clinical trials
health-biotech.summary: Federal health officials have announced a new pilot program designed to accelerate drug development in early-stage clinical trials, part of broader efforts to enhance competitiveness with international markets. The initiative represents a significant shift in FDA regulatory approach to expedite the path from laboratory research to human testing.

health-biotech.title: HHS launches broad effort to expand and speed up clinical trials
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration is implementing a sweeping initiative across the Department of Health and Human Services to modernize and expedite clinical trial processes in response to industry calls for reform. The effort signals a major policy commitment to accelerating drug development timelines across the biotech and pharmaceutical sectors.

health-biotech.title: Closely watched Pfizer lung cancer drug falls short in clinical trial
health-biotech.summary: Pfizer announced that an experimental drug intended to replace a widely-used chemotherapy for one of the most common forms of lung cancer failed to meet its trial endpoints. The setback represents a significant disappointment for the pharmaceutical company's oncology pipeline and the broader lung cancer treatment landscape.

health-biotech.title: AbbVie to buy Apogee Therapeutics in nearly $11B deal
health-biotech.summary: AbbVie announced the acquisition of Apogee Therapeutics for approximately $11 billion, acquiring the developer of an experimental atopic dermatitis drug and continuing a robust wave of pharmaceutical industry consolidation. The deal represents AbbVie's largest post-Allergan acquisition and reflects strong investor confidence in immunology-focused drug candidates.

health-biotech.title: Definium LSD therapy helped patients with major depression in late-stage trial
health-biotech.summary: Definium Therapeutics reported successful Phase 3 trial results for its LSD-based therapy in treating major depressive disorder, bringing a psychedelic drug candidate closer to potential FDA approval. The positive trial outcome marks a significant milestone in the emerging field of psychedelic-assisted psychiatry and represents a major validation of the company's clinical approach.

health-biotech.title: Merck's IBD drug from $10.8B Prometheus deal claims its first Phase 3 win
health-biotech.summary: Merck announced that the lead program from its $10.8 billion acquisition of Prometheus Biosciences achieved its primary endpoint in a Phase 3 trial for inflammatory bowel disease, demonstrating early returns on the investment. The successful trial result validates Merck's strategic rationale for the significant acquisition and positions the drug candidate for potential regulatory advancement.

health-biotech.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo outbreak top 1,000 with 254 deaths, authorities say
health-biotech.summary: Confirmed Ebola cases in the eastern Congo outbreak have exceeded 1,000 with 254 deaths reported, as public health authorities continue to face significant challenges in contact tracing amid ongoing civil unrest. The escalating epidemic represents a serious public health crisis requiring sustained international attention and resources.

health-biotech.title: FDA reverses course on Regenxbio's childhood gene therapy after rejection
health-biotech.summary: The FDA signaled a reversal in its position on Regenxbio's experimental gene therapy for a rare and deadly childhood brain disorder, agreeing to reconsider the application just four months after an initial rejection. The FDA's apparent change in approach suggests leadership transitions and revised regulatory analysis of the therapy's risk-benefit profile.

health-biotech.title: AbbVie makes $10.9B move for Apogee's long-acting I&I drug candidates
health-biotech.summary: AbbVie announced a $10.9 billion acquisition of immunology biotech Apogee Therapeutics to secure long-acting interleukin-13 targeted drug candidates, including zumilokibart which is positioned to enter Phase 3 trials for eczema. The transaction underscores continued industry momentum in immunology and dermatology-focused acquisitions.

health-biotech.title: Despite transplant referrals, most kidney patients don't make the waitlist
health-biotech.summary: A new analysis indicates that the majority of kidney failure patients who receive transplant referrals do not ultimately reach the waiting list, revealing significant gaps in the patient pathway to organ transplantation. The findings highlight systemic barriers and underscore the need for improved processes to facilitate access to life-saving renal transplants.

startups-vc.title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia's $20B not-acqui-hire deal
startups-vc.summary: Groq has secured $650 million in funding and is restructuring its leadership team following Nvidia's acquisition of its talent. The company is pivoting to focus on its neocloud business while bringing on new executives to lead its next phase of growth.

startups-vc.title: Robotics Startups On Fire As Venture Funding Surges To Record Numbers In 2026
startups-vc.summary: Global robotics startups have raised $18.8 billion in 2026 to date, significantly outpacing the $15 billion raised in all of 2025 and surpassing the previous peak funding year of 2021. With more than six months remaining in the year, robotics continues to be one of the most actively funded sectors in venture capital.

startups-vc.title: AppsFlyer Reportedly Lands $1B At $2.7B Valuation To Help Companies Track Digital Ads
startups-vc.summary: Mobile data analytics company AppsFlyer has secured over $1 billion in Series E funding at a $2.7 billion valuation, positioning the company for a potential public market debut. The capital infusion underscores strong investor appetite for marketing and attribution technology platforms.

startups-vc.title: WhatsApp gets new chief as Meta taps India's CRED founder Kunal Shah and invests $900M in startup
startups-vc.summary: Meta has appointed Kunal Shah, founder of Indian fintech CRED, as the new WhatsApp chief, replacing Will Cathcart who is moving to a different role within Meta. Simultaneously, Meta has invested $900 million in a startup, reflecting the company's continued strategic investments in emerging markets and key platforms.

startups-vc.title: European Investor Seedcamp Closes On $320M Across Two Funds To Back Seed Startups And Reaches $1B AUM
startups-vc.summary: Seedcamp, a prominent European seed-stage investor, has closed two new funds totaling $320 million, consisting of a $220 million core fund and a $100 million select fund. The milestone brings Seedcamp's total assets under management to $1 billion, reinforcing its position as a leading seed investor in Europe.

startups-vc.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
startups-vc.summary: Fusion energy startups have collectively raised $7.1 billion to date, with the majority of capital concentrated among a select group of high-funded companies. The analysis provides a comprehensive overview of major players in the fusion sector as investor interest in alternative energy technology continues to grow.

startups-vc.title: SaaS Isn't Coming Back. Something Much Bigger Is Replacing It
startups-vc.summary: Industry observers predict that AI-native, vertical-specific platforms will supersede traditional horizontal SaaS products as the next dominant software category. Vertical AI companies with proprietary data, domain expertise, and established customer relationships are positioned to create sustainable competitive advantages that generalist SaaS offerings cannot match.

startups-vc.title: The US banned Anthropic's Fable 5 release, but the numbers don't seem to care
startups-vc.summary: The U.S. government compelled Anthropic to withdraw its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly identified guardrail bypasses. Despite the regulatory action, cybersecurity experts have challenged the decision, noting that similar vulnerabilities exist in competing AI models.

startups-vc.title: He made your free video player run smoothly. Now he's doing that for robots.
startups-vc.summary: French serial entrepreneur Jean-Baptiste Kempf, known for his work on open-source video technology, is building Kyber, an infrastructure platform for real-time remote device control. The venture addresses a critical gap in robotics infrastructure and leverages Kempf's extensive background in distributed systems and optimization.

startups-vc.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
startups-vc.summary: Over 127,000 workers at U.S.-based tech companies were laid off in mass job cuts during 2025, with layoffs continuing into 2026, according to Crunchbase News tracking. The data reflects ongoing workforce adjustments across the technology sector as companies prioritize profitability and operational efficiency.

energy-climate.summary:
Last week, more than 260 researchers convened in Milan to discuss the opportunities, challenges and...
The post Experts: Why carbon removal needs a ‘major scale up’ to return warming to 1.5C appeared first on Carbon Brief.

energy-climate.title: Canadian Solar launches TOPCon high-power-density module with 24.8% efficiency
energy-climate.summary:
The new modules deliver up to 670 W with a 90% bifaciality factor, yielding a 1.4% LCOE reduction for utility-scale and commercial projects, said the company.
The post Canadian Solar launches TOPCon high-power-density module with 24.8% efficiency  appeared first on pv magazine Global.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A significant data extortion attack targeting Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupted classes and coursework across the United States after attackers defaced the service's login page with ransom demands. The cybercriminals threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty members across nearly 9,000 educational institutions, representing an unprecedented threat to the education sector's operational continuity and privacy.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: High-profile Instagram accounts, including those of the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force, were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian content after attackers exploited a vulnerability in Meta's AI support assistant to reset account passwords. The incident demonstrates how AI-powered customer support systems can become attack vectors when improperly secured, raising concerns about authentication mechanisms across major social platforms.

cybersecurity.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group 'The Gentlemen?'
cybersecurity.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware gang has rapidly emerged as the second most active ransomware group by victim count through an aggressive recruitment strategy offering affiliates 90 percent of ransoms paid. Krebs on Security investigation provides analysis of evidence pointing to the real-world identity of the group's administrator, shedding light on the organized structure behind one of the most prolific ransomware operations.

cybersecurity.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
cybersecurity.summary: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack when threat actors tampered with the vendor's build and distribution pipeline to inject backdoor code into official licensed updates. The attack represents a significant risk to WordPress users relying on these plugins, demonstrating how compromised legitimate update channels can distribute malware at scale.

cybersecurity.title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers disclosed four critical vulnerabilities in Dify, a popular open-source AI workflow platform with over 146,000 GitHub stars, that could allow unauthenticated attackers to read AI conversations from other customers' applications. The flaws, collectively named DifyTap, represent a serious multi-tenant isolation failure that could expose sensitive AI-driven business communications and interactions.

cybersecurity.title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
cybersecurity.summary: A new malware family called AryStinger has infected at least 4,300 legacy home routers to build a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network for pre-attack reconnaissance rather than traditional DDoS botnets. The discovery highlights how abandoned legacy network devices remain attractive targets for attackers seeking infrastructure for surveillance and espionage operations.

cybersecurity.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
cybersecurity.summary: A critical heap over-read vulnerability in the Squid web proxy, dubbed Squidbleed, can leak another user's cleartext HTTP requests including credentials and session tokens to any user with proxy access. The vulnerability stems from a 1997 FTP-parsing code change and remains active in Squid's default configuration, posing significant risks to organizations using the proxy for traffic isolation.

cybersecurity.title: INTERPOL Warns Phishing, Ransomware, and AI Scams Are Rising Across Asia-Pacific
cybersecurity.summary: INTERPOL's 2025/2026 Asia and South Pacific Cyberthreat Assessment Report documents a dramatic increase in cybercrime driven by rapid digitalization, organized criminal networks, and disparities in cybersecurity maturity, with phishing identified as the most widespread threat. The report underscores the growing convergence of traditional attack methods with emerging AI-based scams across the region.

cybersecurity.title: WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
cybersecurity.summary: An ongoing malware campaign is targeting WhatsApp users across multiple countries with deceptive messages containing VBScript files disguised as business documents, leading to remote system compromise. The campaign demonstrates the continued exploitation of trusted messaging platforms and social engineering tactics to deliver malware with full system access.

cybersecurity.title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
cybersecurity.summary: Attackers are exploiting multiple legitimate online platforms including GitHub, YouTube, and VirusTotal to build false credibility and distribute a cross-platform clipboard hijacker targeting cryptocurrency users. The sophisticated social engineering approach demonstrates how threat actors can weaponize trust indicators across multiple channels to compromise financial assets.

asia-pacific.title: North Korea’s Kim says ‘defeated’ Japan transforming into a ‘war state’
asia-pacific.summary:
North Korean leader Kim Jong-un criticised Japan as turning into a “war state”, in a speech during a three-day meeting held through Monday, official news media said on Tuesday.
“Japan, a defeated country in Asia, has openly turned itself into a war state by taking the present disturbing circumstances as an opportunity to get rid of all shackles restricting its moves to become a military power,” he said, according to the Korean Central News Agency (KCNA).
In the concluding speech during a meeting

asia-pacific.title: China zoo offers US$15,000 annual salary for bear costume performer with strict silence clause
asia-pacific.summary:
A recruitment advertisement from a zoo in central China seeking individuals to pose as black bears, offering an annual salary of 100,000 yuan (US$15,000), has gone viral on social media, generating a wave of humorous responses among internet users.
Luohe Wildlife Zoo, located in Luohe city in Henan province, posted the job ad on June 13, looking to hire people to wear black bear costumes, roam the zoo, and interact with visitors, according to Jiupai News.
It is unclear if the costumes come with.

asia-pacific.title: Japan’s visa fee jump to hit Chinese tourists hardest, adding friction to tense ties
asia-pacific.summary:
Chinese tourists are expected to be among the hardest hit by Japan’s fivefold visa fee increase, a move analysts warned could further strain already tense relations between Beijing and Tokyo.
For the first time since 1978, the Japanese government has decided to raise visa fees. The cost of a single-entry visa will increase from 3,000 yen (US$18.54) to 15,000 yen, while multiple-entry visas will jump from 6,000 yen to 30,000 yen.
The new policy, which will come into effect on July 1, has been...

asia-pacific.title: The I Ching, Leibniz and AI: how old China-West links shaped modern science
asia-pacific.summary:
The advent of artificial intelligence (AI) appears sudden and revolutionary, yet its origins stretch deep into history, revealing a profound and forgotten intellectual exchange between China and the West.
Long before Alan Turing, Claude Shannon, or even Charles Babbage and Ada Lovelace, there was Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz – the German polymath born exactly 380 years ago next week. Among his monumental contributions to science is the binary numeral system, which serves as the bedrock of modern...

asia-pacific.title: Competing for tourists? Then Hong Kong must be ‘bigger and better’: Rosanna Law
asia-pacific.summary:
As Hong Kong marks the 29th anniversary of its return to Chinese rule on July 1, the South China Morning Post talks to the city’s senior officials about the administration’s achievements so far and what may lie ahead.
Hong Kong should focus on becoming “bigger and better”, rather than expecting neighbouring cities such as Shenzhen to “stand still”, the city’s tourism chief has said, as the government prepares its first five-year plan and seeks to raise the sector’s contribution to the...

asia-pacific.title: Indian man jailed for molesting flight attendant on Singapore Airlines plane
asia-pacific.summary:
A passenger on a Singapore Airlines flight molested an air stewardess, with his friends laughing in response.
When the woman went to report the incident to her supervisor, the culprit followed her into the galley, stood extremely close to her and cornered her.
The flight captain reported the incident and a police report was lodged, leading to the man’s arrest when he arrived at Changi Airport.
Akash Tiwari, a 35-year-old Indian national, was sentenced to six months’ jail on Monday. He was also..

asia-pacific.title: Why South Korea’s AI chip boom is a ‘serious concern’ for its economy
asia-pacific.summary:
South Korea’s AI-driven semiconductor boom has sent exports, corporate profits and stock prices to record highs, but a senior policymaker has warned that the windfall could fuel property speculation and deepen inequality if its gains remain narrowly concentrated.
“Looking solely at the numbers, it is something to cheer about. However, strangely, a corner of my heart feels heavy,” Kim Yong-beom, chief of the Presidential Policy Office and one of South Korea’s most senior economic policymakers,...

asia-pacific.title: How Keir Starmer will be replaced as UK PM and will anyone stand against Burnham?
asia-pacific.summary:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer conceded on Monday that he had lost the support of his rank-and-file Labour Party members in Parliament and that he will step down once his successor as party leader is chosen, possibly as soon as the middle of July.
Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester whose resounding victory in a special UK election last week set in motion Starmer’s resignation, has confirmed he will put himself forward to be his successor.
Burnham is seen as the...

asia-pacific.title: Former Gotti hitman-turned-New Jersey councillor has returned to crime, prosecutors allege
asia-pacific.summary:
A notorious mob hitman who once testified against John “Junior” Gotti before cleaning up his life and becoming a councilman in New Jersey has been arrested on extortion and loan sharking charges that, if proven, reflect a return to the lifestyle of his youth.
John Alite, 63, was arrested on Friday in New Jersey, where he was sworn in early last year as a councillor in the borough of Englishtown. Released after a court appearance on Saturday, Alite is scheduled to return to court for a detention.

asia-pacific.title: Trump criticises UK’s Starmer after PM announces resignation
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump criticised outgoing British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday after the Labour leader announced he would resign, saying he had “really hurt himself” over energy, immigration and his handling of relations with Washington.
“I think he’s a lovely man,” Trump told reporters at an Oval Office event, before accusing Starmer of mishandling Britain’s energy policy by failing to exploit North Sea oil and allowing “windmills all over the place”.
“The UK buys much of its...
